persona.name: Diane Caldwell

occupation.title: High School PE Teacher & Head Track Coach
occupation.description: Teaches physical education to 9th-12th graders and coaches the varsity track and cross-country teams. On her feet from 7 AM to 5 PM during the season. Manages equipment budgets, parent communications, and college recruitment referrals for top athletes.

persona.education: B.S. in Kinesiology from Ball State University. Master's in Physical Education from Indiana University. Former NCAA Division II 800m runner.
long_term_goals: Stay running competitively into her 50s through masters events
Write a practical guide for high school running coaches
Help her athletes earn college scholarships
Retire at 55 and run full-time masters meets
persona.style: Diane communicates like a coach: clear, structured, encouraging but honest. She speaks with authority built on 20+ years of running and coaching experience. She uses technical terminology correctly but explains it when she senses the listener is lost. She has no tolerance for marketing BS — she's fitted hundreds of student athletes and knows what works. Dresses in school athletic gear most days, owns exactly two pairs of 'nice' shoes.

traits: Authoritative and experienced — trusts her own judgment over marketing
Nurturing with students but no-nonsense with adults
Knowledgeable about biomechanics, gait, and shoe construction
Frugal with personal spending but advocates for quality
Skeptical of trends — prefers proven models and designs
Recommends shoes to parents weekly — her opinion carries weight

likes: Shoes with proven track records over multiple model years
Brands that support high school athletics programs
Shoes available in both racing and training versions
Neutral trainers that work for varied foot types
Discount codes for coaches and educators
Simple, durable designs that hold up to daily use
dislikes: Brands that change successful models unnecessarily
Shoes that look fast but fall apart in 3 months
Marketing that targets elite runners and ignores the 99%
Products with no information about stack height or drop
Trendy colorways that distract from function
Brands that don't offer team pricing for schools

beliefs: The right shoe prevents injuries — she's seen bad shoes derail promising athletes
You don't need expensive shoes to run well, but you need the right ones
Coaching is about developing the whole person, not just the athlete
Experience with hundreds of runners gives her better shoe insight than any review site
skills: Gait analysis and shoe fitting for teen athletes
Track and cross-country training program design
Sports injury prevention and first aid
Budget management for school athletic programs

general: Runs 5 days a week — 3 easy, 1 tempo, 1 long — year-round
Keeps a spreadsheet of shoes she's recommended to athletes and parents
Visits the local running store twice a year to see what's new
Wears shoes for at least 400 miles before judging them

workday: Meet with athletes or parents after practice
evening: Watch race footage (her athletes or pro meets)
weekend: Watch meets or drive athletes to invitational

health.physical: Strong for her age but feeling the accumulated miles. Right Achilles tendon gets stiff after hard workouts. Mild meniscus wear in left knee from years of track surfaces. Managing perimenopause symptoms that affect sleep and recovery. Still competitive in masters 800m events.
health.mental: Motivated and purposeful. Coaching gives her daily meaning. Occasionally stressed during championship season. Worries about aging out of competitive running but channels it into coaching excellence.

relationships.name: Tom Caldwell
relationships.description: Works in IT at Eli Lilly. Not a runner. Supports her coaching schedule and handles dinner on practice nights.

relationships.name: Emma Caldwell
relationships.description: Freshman at Purdue studying engineering. Was a decent sprinter in high school but chose not to run in college. Diane respects the decision.

relationships.name: Coach Ray Simmons
relationships.description: Assistant track coach. Former D1 hurdler. They debate shoe brands in the equipment room.

relationships.name: Maria Santos
relationships.description: Middle school PE teacher who runs masters meets. They train together on Saturdays.
